name: envelope-discipline
description: Envelope Discipline (futures-land-as-fields) — a standalone, self-obeying framework for HOW a seam's configurables are shaped at the moment the seam is minted. Models interface calcification as (first-of-family mistaken for the whole story) × (no envelope reserved at the only cheap moment), targets the fixable factor, and gates on what is about to be MINTED rather than on how small the knob looks: the first option reveals the family and gets one named options envelope at birth; defaults are the null ask (silent, deny, bare) so no default carries doctrine and nothing derives desired functionality from an artifact's presence; futures land as new named fields, never as signature breaks, accreted positional scalars, or per-knob rulings; and the family shape propagates through every layer of the seam — wire grammar included — or the flat scalar has just moved down one. Twelve rules in three groups, three mottos, and the live minting failure they were derived from. Shape-side sibling of verification-discipline (what may be believed) and emission-discipline (what may be released): this framework gates the contracts the other two operate across. Standalone; no external plugin dependencies.
